title: Leucanthemopsis radicans
taxonomy: Leucanthemopsis radicans is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Leucanthemopsis and is also known as Alpine Daisy or Dwarf Daisy. The classification of this species can be broken down further, with it being placed in the tribe Gnaphalieae. Within the tribe, Leucanthemopsis radicans is part of the subtribe Gnaphaliinae. Further classification places it in the family Asteraceae, specifically in the clade Campanulidae.
uses: Leucanthemopsis radicans has been traditionally used in some parts of the world for its potential medicinal properties. It has been used to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. intro: Leucanthemopsis radicans, commonly known as Alpine Daisy, is a low-growing, mat-forming alpine plant native to the mountains of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemopsis radicans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Alpine Daisy is traditionally used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of bloating and indigestion.
